element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Collect at least 10 cargo boxes per level to open exit
Players can switch between Baloo and Kit for different level approaches
Alternative gameplay controlling the aircraft pulling Kit
Secondary objective layered over platform progression

typical_relief_triggers: Collecting 10th cargo box to open exit
typical_tension_triggers: Cargo box scarcity in level sections
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Collection-driven progression with escalating pirate threat; alternates between exploration pressure (cargo hunting) and combat avoidance

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Cargo boxes scattered throughout levels as both progression gates and exploration rewards

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to cargo collection mechanic and basic platforming; manageable enemy density
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased air pirate encounters; artifact fragments require exploration; bonus level sequences introduce piloting mechanics
difficulty_curve.late_game: Contest escalation against Shere Khan's forces; higher density of obstacles and enemies defending cargo locations
notable_spikes: Sea Duck bonus level (new control scheme)
Final contest levels with combined platforming and combat pressure

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear progression through level space while collecting scattered cargo boxes; minimum 10 required to unlock exit. Encourages thorough exploration and revisiting areas.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic level structure (all levels follow this core pattern)

notable_level_patterns.description: Sections where air pirates create obstacle courses that players must navigate or avoid to reach cargo-rich areas
Mid-game shift to alternative gameplay (Sea Duck piloting sequences) that breaks from standard platforming rhythm
